+# ============================================================================
+# GNU screen compatibility for tmux
+# ============================================================================
+# Makes tmux behave like screen with default keybindings
+# After 20 years of muscle memory, this config translates screen to tmux
+#
+# Usage: Place this file at ~/.tmux.conf and reload tmux (Ctrl-a r)
+# Quick start: tmux new -s mysession
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Set prefix to Ctrl-a (screen's default) instead of Ctrl-b
+unbind C-b
+set -g prefix C-a
+bind C-a send-prefix
+
+# Window management (match screen behavior)
+bind c new-window                           # Ctrl-a c: create window
+bind C-c new-window                         # Ctrl-a Ctrl-c: create window
+bind n next-window                          # Ctrl-a n: next window
+bind C-n next-window                        # Ctrl-a Ctrl-n: next window
+bind BSpace previous-window                 # Ctrl-a Backspace: previous window
+bind p previous-window                      # Ctrl-a p: previous window
+bind C-p previous-window                    # Ctrl-a Ctrl-p: previous window
+bind C-a last-window                        # Ctrl-a Ctrl-a: toggle last window
+bind l last-window                          # Ctrl-a l: toggle last window (screen alternative)
+bind a send-keys C-a                        # Ctrl-a a: send literal Ctrl-a
+bind A command-prompt "rename-window '%%'" # Ctrl-a A: rename window
+bind , command-prompt "rename-window '%%'" # Ctrl-a ,: rename window (alternative)
+bind N command-prompt "select-window -t '%%'"  # Ctrl-a N: select window by number/name
+bind '"' choose-window                      # Ctrl-a ": list windows
+bind w choose-window                        # Ctrl-a w: list windows (alternative)
+bind W choose-window                        # Ctrl-a W: list windows (alternative)
+bind "'" command-prompt -p "window number or name:" "select-window -t '%%'"  # Ctrl-a ': switch to window
+bind . command-prompt "move-window -t '%%'" # Ctrl-a .: move window to new number
+bind - last-window                          # Ctrl-a -: toggle last window (alternative)
+bind k confirm-before -p "kill-window #W? (y/n)" kill-window  # Ctrl-a k: kill window
+bind C-k confirm-before -p "kill-window #W? (y/n)" kill-window  # Ctrl-a Ctrl-k: kill window
+bind K confirm-before -p "kill-window #W? (y/n)" kill-window  # Ctrl-a K: kill window
+bind '\' confirm-before -p "kill-session #S? (y/n)" kill-session  # Ctrl-a \: kill session
+bind C-'\' confirm-before -p "kill-session #S? (y/n)" kill-session  # Ctrl-a Ctrl-\: kill session
+bind 0 select-window -t :0                  # Ctrl-a 0-9: select window by number
+bind 1 select-window -t :1
+bind 2 select-window -t :2
+bind 3 select-window -t :3
+bind 4 select-window -t :4
+bind 5 select-window -t :5
+bind 6 select-window -t :6
+bind 7 select-window -t :7
+bind 8 select-window -t :8
+bind 9 select-window -t :9
+
+# Session management
+bind d detach-client                        # Ctrl-a d: detach
+bind C-d detach-client                      # Ctrl-a Ctrl-d: detach
+bind D detach-client -P                     # Ctrl-a D: power detach (detach and logout)
+bind s choose-session                       # Ctrl-a s: choose session
+bind C-z suspend-client                     # Ctrl-a Ctrl-z: suspend tmux client
+
+# Copy mode (scrollback)
+bind [ copy-mode                            # Ctrl-a [: enter copy mode
+bind C-[ copy-mode                          # Ctrl-a Ctrl-[: enter copy mode
+bind Escape copy-mode                       # Ctrl-a Esc: enter copy mode (screen alternative)
+bind ] paste-buffer                         # Ctrl-a ]: paste
+bind > command-prompt -p "save buffer to:" "save-buffer '%%'"  # Ctrl-a >: save buffer to file
+bind < command-prompt -p "load buffer from:" "load-buffer '%%'"  # Ctrl-a <: load buffer from file
+
+# Pane splitting (screen uses S for horizontal split)
+bind S split-window                         # Ctrl-a S: split horizontal
+bind | split-window -h                      # Ctrl-a |: split vertical
+bind V split-window -h                      # Ctrl-a V: split vertical (alternative)
+bind Tab select-pane -t :.+                 # Ctrl-a Tab: cycle panes
+bind BTab select-pane -t :.-                # Ctrl-a Shift-Tab: cycle panes backwards
+bind o select-pane -t :.+                   # Ctrl-a o: cycle panes (alternative)
+bind Space next-layout                      # Ctrl-a Space: cycle through layouts
+bind Q kill-pane -a                         # Ctrl-a Q: kill all panes except current
+bind X kill-pane                            # Ctrl-a X: kill current pane
+bind F resize-pane -Z                       # Ctrl-a F: focus (zoom) pane
+
+# Pane navigation with arrow keys
+bind -r Up select-pane -U                   # Ctrl-a Up: select pane above
+bind -r Down select-pane -D                 # Ctrl-a Down: select pane below
+bind -r Left select-pane -L                 # Ctrl-a Left: select pane left
+bind -r Right select-pane -R                # Ctrl-a Right: select pane right
+
+# Pane resizing
+bind -r C-Up resize-pane -U 1               # Ctrl-a Ctrl-Up: resize pane up
+bind -r C-Down resize-pane -D 1             # Ctrl-a Ctrl-Down: resize pane down
+bind -r C-Left resize-pane -L 1             # Ctrl-a Ctrl-Left: resize pane left
+bind -r C-Right resize-pane -R 1            # Ctrl-a Ctrl-Right: resize pane right
+
+# Help and info
+bind ? list-keys                            # Ctrl-a ?: show keybindings
+bind i display-message                      # Ctrl-a i: display window info
+bind * list-clients                         # Ctrl-a *: list clients
+bind t clock-mode                           # Ctrl-a t: display clock
+bind v display-message "tmux #{version}"    # Ctrl-a v: display version
+
+# Window monitoring
+bind M set-window-option monitor-activity   # Ctrl-a M: monitor window for activity
+bind _ set-window-option monitor-silence 30 # Ctrl-a _: monitor window for 30s silence
+
+# Redraw and clear
+bind C-l refresh-client                     # Ctrl-a Ctrl-l: refresh client
+bind C clear-history                        # Ctrl-a C: clear screen and history
+
+# Command mode
+bind : command-prompt                       # Ctrl-a :: command prompt
+
+# Locking (requires lock-command to be set)
+bind x confirm-before -p "kill-pane #P? (y/n)" kill-pane  # Ctrl-a x: kill pane (with confirmation)
+
+# Copy/paste buffer management
+bind = choose-buffer                        # Ctrl-a =: choose which buffer to paste
+
+# Misc
+bind m command-prompt -p "monitor-content:" "set-window-option monitor-content '%%'"  # Ctrl-a m: monitor for content
+bind H pipe-pane -o "cat >>~/tmux-#W.log" \; display "Toggled logging to ~/tmux-#W.log"  # Ctrl-a H: toggle logging
+bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display "Reloaded ~/.tmux.conf"  # Ctrl-a r: reload config
+bind z resize-pane -Z                       # Ctrl-a z: zoom pane (toggle fullscreen)
+bind f command-prompt "find-window '%%'"    # Ctrl-a f: find window by name
+bind { swap-pane -U                         # Ctrl-a {: swap pane with previous
+bind } swap-pane -D                         # Ctrl-a }: swap pane with next
+bind ! break-pane                           # Ctrl-a !: break pane into window
+bind @ command-prompt -p "create pane from window:" "join-pane -s '%%'"  # Ctrl-a @: join pane from window
+bind # list-buffers                         # Ctrl-a #: list buffers
+bind - delete-buffer                        # Ctrl-a -: delete last buffer
+bind + delete-buffer -b 0                   # Ctrl-a +: delete buffer 0
+bind ~ show-messages                        # Ctrl-a ~: show messages
+bind PPage copy-mode -u                     # Ctrl-a PageUp: enter copy mode and scroll up
+bind q display-panes                        # Ctrl-a q: display pane numbers
+bind ; last-pane                            # Ctrl-a ;: select last pane
+
+# Screen-like status bar
+set -g status-style bg=blue,fg=white
+set -g status-left ''
+set -g status-right '#[fg=white]#H'
+set -g window-status-format '#I:#W#F'
+set -g window-status-current-format '#I:#W#F'
+set -g window-status-current-style bg=white,fg=blue,bold
+
+# Start window numbering at 1 (like screen)
+set -g base-index 1
+set -g pane-base-index 1
+
+# Enable mouse support (useful addition, disable if you want pure screen behavior)
+set -g mouse on
+
+# Increase scrollback buffer
+set -g history-limit 10000
+
+# Vi mode for copy mode (screen default is similar)
+setw -g mode-keys vi
+
+# Screen-like behavior settings
+set -g renumber-windows on                  # Renumber windows when one is closed
+set -g allow-rename on                      # Allow programs to change window name
+set -g automatic-rename on                  # Automatically set window title
+set -g display-panes-time 2000              # Display pane indicators for 2 seconds
+set -g display-time 2000                    # Display messages for 2 seconds
+set -sg escape-time 0                       # No delay for escape key press
+set -g focus-events on                      # Enable focus events
+set -g set-titles on                        # Set terminal title
+set -g set-titles-string "#S:#I:#W - \"#T\" #{session_alerts}"  # Terminal title format
+
+# Activity monitoring
+setw -g monitor-activity on                 # Monitor for activity in windows
+set -g visual-activity off                  # Don't display activity messages in status line
+set -g visual-bell off                      # Don't show visual bell
+set -g bell-action any                      # Bell in any window triggers attention
+
+# Copy mode vi bindings (screen-like behavior)
+bind -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection                    # v: begin selection
+bind -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-selection-and-cancel          # y: copy and exit
+bind -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle                   # r: toggle rectangle mode
+bind -T copy-mode-vi Escape send-keys -X cancel                        # Esc: exit copy mode
+bind -T copy-mode-vi / send-keys -X search-forward                     # /: search forward
+bind -T copy-mode-vi ? send-keys -X search-backward                    # ?: search backward
+bind -T copy-mode-vi n send-keys -X search-again                       # n: next search result
+bind -T copy-mode-vi N send-keys -X search-reverse                     # N: previous search result
+bind -T copy-mode-vi Space send-keys -X page-down                      # Space: page down
+bind -T copy-mode-vi C-b send-keys -X page-up                          # Ctrl-b: page up
+bind -T copy-mode-vi C-d send-keys -X halfpage-down                    # Ctrl-d: half page down
+bind -T copy-mode-vi C-u send-keys -X halfpage-up                      # Ctrl-u: half page up
+bind -T copy-mode-vi g send-keys -X history-top                        # g: go to top
+bind -T copy-mode-vi G send-keys -X history-bottom                     # G: go to bottom
+bind -T copy-mode-vi h send-keys -X cursor-left                        # h: move left
+bind -T copy-mode-vi j send-keys -X cursor-down                        # j: move down
+bind -T copy-mode-vi k send-keys -X cursor-up                          # k: move up
+bind -T copy-mode-vi l send-keys -X cursor-right                       # l: move right
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Optional customizations
+# ============================================================================
+# Uncomment these if you want additional improvements over screen defaults:
+
+# Better colors (uncomment if you want 256 color support)
+# set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
+# set -ga terminal-overrides ",xterm-256color:Tc"
+
+# Disable mouse (uncomment for pure keyboard-only like screen)
+# set -g mouse off
+
+# Longer status bar with more info (uncomment to enable)
+# set -g status-left '[#S] '
+# set -g status-right '#{?client_prefix,#[reverse] PREFIX #[noreverse] ,}"#H" %H:%M %d-%b-%y'
+# set -g status-left-length 20
+# set -g status-right-length 50
+
+# ============================================================================
+# End of configuration
+# ============================================================================
